mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2014 payroll level at 137,318 thousand persons indicates ongoing post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k jobs.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 2.0 percent while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable, the real interest rate becomes negative, incentivizing equity investment over cash and bonds. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as portfolio reallocation accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2014 FEDFUNDS at 1.81% and CPIAUCSL at 218.88 satisfy trigger condition
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between trigger activation and forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 900,000 annualized units signal strong residential investment demand and forward-looking builder confidence. This demand expansion increases multiplier effects through construction employment and materials consumption, supporting equity returns over the next 5 trading days through near-term sentiment shifts.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2014 HOUST at 895.0 thousand units, near trigger threshold
regime_hint: housing recovery phase in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher in months where HOUST > 900k versus HOUST < 850k (t-test p > 0.15), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while unemployment remains elevated above 6.0%, signaling slack in the economy and reduced near-term inflation risk. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as growth concerns dominate inflation fears.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2014 INDPRO at 110.30 and UNRATE at 6.1% indicate slack conditions
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with persistent labor market slack
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (INDPRO × UNRATE) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while housing starts simultaneously exceed 900,000 units, indicating broad-based consumer and residential demand strength. This joint interaction mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as synchronized demand expansion supports corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST > 900 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:April 2014 RSAFS at 375,473 million dollars and HOUST at 895.0 thousand units show demand momentum
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion in late-cycle recovery
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher when both conditions are met versus when either is absent (t-test p > 0.12), or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate above 2.5% signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity discount rates rise and growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2014 FEDFUNDS at 1.81% remains below tightening threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window, signaling accelerating labor market tightening and reduced slack. This trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as tighter labor markets support wage growth and consumer confidence.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2014 UNRATE at 6.1% reflects ongoing labor market normalization from crisis lows
regime_hint: labor market tightening in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher in periods of rapid UNRATE decline versus stable UNRATE (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 0.4% month-over-month while the unemployment rate simultaneously falls below 6.0%, signaling synchronized supply-side expansion and labor market tightening. This interaction mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as near-term growth momentum and tight labor conditions drive sentiment.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2014 INDPRO at 110.30 and UNRATE at 6.1% approach trigger conditions
regime_hint: supply-side acceleration with labor market tightening
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher when both conditions are met versus when either is absent (t-test p > 0.15), or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
